solution: def isPowerOfFour(self, n: int) -> bool:
    return n > 0 and (n & (n - 1)) == 0 and (n // 4) == int(n // 4)

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(1)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW

Explanation:
The solution uses bit manipulation to check if a number is a power of four without using loops or recursion. The algorithm works by checking if the number is greater than 0, has no trailing ones in its binary representation (checked using the bitwise AND operator with (n - 1)), and has the same integer division by 4 as the floor division by 4 (checked using the // operator). If all these conditions are true, the number is a power of four. The solution has a constant time complexity (O(1)) and constant space complexity (O(1)).
code:
def isPowerOfFour(self, n: int) -> bool:
    return n > 0 and (n & (n - 1)) == 0 and (n // 4) == int(n // 4)
